medical_history.history_of_present_illness: Diagnosed with CyN at age one. Multiple hospitalizations for severe infections and required numerous surgeries related to underlying neutropenia. Extensive history of oral mucosa lesions, throat, ear, fingernail and buttock infections, and bouts of bloody diarrhea with colitis. Surgeries included tonsillectomy, mastoidectomy, appendectomy, and cholecystectomy. Neutropenic cycles initially every 25–28 days with 3–5 days of nadir, temporary improvement with filgrastim treatment. Cycles shortened to 15–18 days but remained vulnerable to infections during nadirs and suffered opportunistic infections despite G-CSF, later replaced with PEGylated G-CSF.
medical_history.past_medical_history: Cyclic neutropenia (CyN), familial Mediterranean fever (MEFV) mutation, multiple infections, surgeries (tonsillectomy, mastoidectomy, appendectomy, cholecystectomy).

physical_examination.specialty_examination: Bone marrow biopsy showed myeloid hyperplasia with prominent toxic granulation and a shift with immaturity consistent with G-CSF effect, but with no overt dysplasia and only 1% blasts, and otherwise normal hematopoiesis with no evidence of malignancy.

laboratory_examinations.findings: c.573 597 + 5del, heterozygous, frame-shift deletion mutation of neutrophil elastase
Positive for MEFV with nucleic acid change c. [1105C>T; 1223G>A] and amino acid alteration p. [Pro369Ser; Arg408Gln]

Myeloid hyperplasia with prominent toxic granulation and a shift with immaturity consistent with G-CSF effect, but with no overt dysplasia and only 1% blasts, and otherwise normal hematopoiesis with no evidence of malignancy

laboratory_examinations.changes_over_time: Decreased to 4.9 (1000/uL) on Day -2, <0.1 (1000/uL) on Day +7, 2.9 (1000/uL) on Day +27 and Day +41, and 5.7 (1000/uL) on Day +78
Decreased to 4.90 (1000/uL) on Day -2, too few on Day +7, 1.50 (1000/uL) on Day +27, 1.80 (1000/uL) on Day +41, and 5.50 (1000/uL) on Day +78
Decreased to 10.8 (g/dL) on Day -2, 6.5 (g/dL) on Day +7, 10.6 (g/dL) on Day +27, 7.5 (g/dL) on Day +41, and 11.8 (g/dL) on Day +78
Decreased to 56 (1000/uL) on Day -2, 31 (1000/uL) on Day +7, 227 (1000/uL) on Day +27, 164 (1000/uL) on Day +41, and 77 (1000/uL) on Day +78

other_tests.procedure_name: Bone marrow biopsy (core and aspirate) post-transplant
other_tests.findings: Engrafting bone marrow with trilineage hematopoiesis and decreased cellularity compared to previous marrow (30% versus 80%)
Granularity and cobble stone appearance in the small bowel, consistent with GI GvHD
Grade 4 of 4 GI GvHD, with extensive mucosal ulceration and development of pneumatosis cystoids intestinalis, acute serositis, and likely focal microperforation

diagnosis.final_diagnosis: ELANE and MEFV mutation positive severe cyclic neutropenia
diagnosis.diagnostic_reasoning: The patient was diagnosed with cyclic neutropenia (CyN) at age one and had a history of multiple severe infections and hospitalizations. Testing revealed a heterozygous frame-shift deletion mutation in the ELANE gene and a positive MEFV mutation. The combination of these mutations was hypothesized to contribute to the severity of symptoms, leading to the decision for allogeneic bone marrow transplantation.

treatment_information.specific_treatment: Allogeneic bone marrow hematopoietic stem cell transplant
Conditioning chemotherapy (cyclophosphamide, fludarabine, anti-thymocyte globulin, low dose total body irradiation)

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: 120 to 150 mg BID, goal trough level 200-250

treatment_information.specific_treatment: Steroids, calcineurin inhibitors, cellcept, budesonide, infliximab

treatment_information.dosage_or_details: Due to perforation of the cecum
